DECLARATION OF TLES. SCALY IN SUPPORT OF HER OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISMISS FILED BY PLAINTIFF SCLAY, TLES.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF TLES. SCLAY PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF THE ORDER FILED JANUARY 23, 2017, DISMISSING THIS CASE AS TO ALL DEFENDANTS IS DENIED. THE MOTION FAILS TO MEET THE REQUIREMENTS FOR A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION PER CCP 1008 AND, EVEN IF THE COURT IGNORED THOSE REQUIREMENTS, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW ANY FACTS OR CIRCUMSTANCES WARRANTING A DIFFERENT RULING ON DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO DISMISS THAT LED TO THE JANUARY 23, 2017 ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG; DEPT. 302.

calendar.matters:
Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration
Off Calendar Mar-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
TLes. SClay, in pro per, for plaintiffs;
Mark D. Fenske, of Vogl Meredith Burke, LLP, Tel. 415.398.0200, for defendants, appearing via CourtCall.

Ruling: A R G U E D. PLAINTIFF TLES. SCLAY Plaintiff's Motion For Reconsideration of the order filed January 23, 2017, dismissing this case as to all defendants is denied. The motion fails to meet the requirements for a motion for reconsideration per CCP 1008 and, even if the court ignored those requirements, plaintiff has failed to show any facts or circumstances warranting a different ruling on defendants' motion to dismiss that led to the January 23, 2017 order. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-01-2017 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance by or on behalf of Plaintiff. Court ordered matter dismissed pursuant to CCP 583.210 and 583.250. Proceeding not reported. (610)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jan-23-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
T Leslie S Clay, aka Tles Sclay, (no contanct number provided) appears In Propria Persona as plaintiff

Attorney, Mark D. Fenske of the law offices of Vogl Meredith Burke LLP (415.398.0200) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendants Marine Humane Society, Cindy Machado, Steve Hill and Adrienne Russo's motion to quash and dismiss action is granted as to the portion of the motion seeking dismissal. The case is dismissed in its entirety. Even if the moving defendants were properly served with a summons and complaint on October 30, 2016, they are still entitled to have this case dismissed per CCP 583.250 due to the failure to serve any of them within three years after this case was filed. None of the arguments made by plaintiff TLes. SClay requires a different result. Ms. SClay has not shown that any of the defendants avoided service or misled her, much less that she actually or justifiably relied on any asserted misrepresentation that is attributable to any of the defendants. Moreover, although service on the County of Marin would not suffice as service on any of the defendants, the court notes that, contrary to the apparent position of Ms. SClay, there is no record in the court's file that service was ever made on the County of Marin. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
